insights.title: NoteOff events from MidiPlayer are invisible to sibling MIDI script processors
insights.summary: A script processor placed in the same MIDI chain as a MidiPlayer (as a sibling) will not receive noteOff callbacks. The note-offs are pushed to the future-event buffer, which is not processed by sibling processors. Place script processors that need noteOff events inside a child container beneath the MidiPlayer.
insights.verify_hint: grep addHiseEventToBuffer in MidiPlayer.cpp; check future-event buffer routing in preprocessBuffer()

insights.title: MIDI files are silently absent from compiled plugins if pooled files are not exported before compiling
insights.summary: MIDI files must be exported via 'Export > Export Pooled Files To Binary Resource' before compiling. If skipped, the midiFilesSize in PresetData.h will be 28 bytes (empty pool) and no files will play in the compiled plugin.

insights.title: LoopEnabled button was broken - loop was always active regardless of toggle state
insights.summary: Christoph Hart confirmed a regression where the LoopEnabled toggle had no effect and the player would always loop. Additionally, disabling the loop caused the first beat to be missed on each iteration. Workaround: use the sequence callback with Math.ceil on NumBars to normalize the loop boundary.
insights.verify_hint: check LoopEnabled handling in preprocessBuffer() MidiPlayer.cpp; check loopEnabled flag path for off-by-one at boundary

insights.title: Sequence length defaults to last note-off position, not last bar boundary
insights.summary: When a MIDI file is loaded, its length is set to the timestamp of the last note-off event. If the file has no end-of-track marker or no notes reaching the final bar boundary, NumBars will be fractional. Round up with Math.ceil via setSequenceCallback to get clean loop boundaries.

insights.title: Use setSequenceCallback to normalize sequence length to full bars on load
insights.summary: Call setSequenceCallback with a function that reads the TimeSignature, rounds NumBars up with Math.ceil, and writes it back via setTimeSignature. This ensures the loop boundary always falls on a bar line regardless of note content.

insights.title: MidiPlayer does not support tempo changes embedded in MIDI files
insights.summary: Tempo map events (ritardando, accelerando) in a MIDI file are ignored. The player is designed for loop-based sequencers that sync to an external DAW clock. Engine.setHostBpm() or PlaybackSpeed can be used for tempo adjustments in standalone mode.

insights.title: MidiPlayer events are artificial; use Message.isArtificial() to separate from user MIDI input
insights.summary: All events injected by MidiPlayer are marked as artificial. In a script processor that receives both player output and live keyboard input, use Message.isArtificial() to distinguish them. Alternatively, assign the MidiPlayer to a different MIDI channel via the CurrentTrack parameter.
insights.verify_hint: grep isArtificial in MidiPlayer.cpp preprocessBuffer(); check HiseEvent artificial flag assignment

insights.title: The keyboard floating tile does not animate from MidiPlayer output
insights.summary: The built-in keyboard FloatingTile responds only to incoming MIDI events, not to the artificial events injected by MidiPlayer. To visualise playback on a keyboard, create a custom panel and use the MidiPlayer's event callbacks or getNoteRectangleList.

insights.title: Calling Engine.getPlayHead() inside audio callbacks causes heap allocation crash
insights.summary: Engine.getPlayHead() allocates a new object each call. Calling it inside a timer or transport callback on the audio thread causes an 'Illegal operation in audio thread: HeapBlock allocation' error. Call it once in onInit and reuse the stored reference; its properties update automatically.

insights.title: Pass an empty string to setFile() to clear all loaded sequences
insights.summary: There is no explicit clearAllSequences() scripting API call. Passing an empty string as the first argument to setFile() — MidiPlayer.setFile("", true, true) — effectively clears all sequences.
insights.verify_hint: check setFile() implementation in MidiPlayer scripting API; look for empty string handling

insights.title: NoteRectangleList indices only align with EventList when filtered to NoteOn events only
insights.summary: getNoteRectangleList() returns one rectangle per note, following NoteOn order. If the EventList contains CC or other non-note events, the indices diverge. Filter the EventList to NoteOn events only before using EventId to index into the rectangle list.

insights.title: Each sequence supports exactly one TimeSignature; multiple time signatures per sequence are not supported
insights.summary: A sequence holds a single TimeSignature object. Files with internal time signature changes will have those changes ignored. The workaround is to split content into multiple sequences and switch between them.

insights.title: Use Synth.getMidiPlayer() for full API; use asMidiPlayer() if you only have a MidiProcessor reference
insights.summary: To access transport methods (play, stop, setFile etc.), obtain a MidiPlayer reference via Synth.getMidiPlayer(). If you already hold a MidiProcessor reference (e.g. from getMidiProcessor()), call .asMidiPlayer() on it to get the full scripting interface.

insights.title: setFile() with {PROJECT_FOLDER} wildcard targets the MidiFiles subfolder in the project
insights.summary: When using {PROJECT_FOLDER} as a path prefix, it resolves to the project's MidiFiles directory. Use file.toString(File.FullPath) when loading externally browsed files via FileSystem.browse, not the File object directly.
